## [1.0.0](https://github.com/awinogradov/code-assistants/compare/release-action@v0.1.0...release-action@v1.0.0) (2026-05-29)

## Release Notes

The release-action now requires explicit opt-in for auto-merging release PRs, standardizes authentication with bot tokens, and improves monorepo release notes with ticket tracking.

## ✨ What's New

### Opt-in auto-merge for release PRs
Release PRs no longer merge automatically by default. Teams can now control whether releases require human approval by adding `release.automerge: true` to their root `package.json`. Without this flag, release PRs will stay approved but unmerged, waiting for manual merge — perfect for teams wanting that final human review before deployment.

<details><summary>Related issues</summary>

- [#112: Gate release auto-merge behind a release.automerge flag in package.json](https://github.com/awinogradov/code-assistants/issues/112)
</details>

### Enhanced monorepo release notes
Monorepo releases now include the same detailed ticket tables as standalone releases, showing GitHub Issues, Linear, and Jira tickets for each package. The ticket tables are automatically scoped to each member package's commits and use your existing release credentials — no additional configuration needed.

<details><summary>Related issues</summary>

- [#102: Monorepo release notes are missing the GitHub Issues / Linear / Jira blocks](https://github.com/awinogradov/code-assistants/issues/102)
</details>

## 🐛 Bug Fixes

### Bot identity resolution
The action now properly retrieves bot user IDs from GitHub's API when generating noreply email addresses, ensuring commits show correct authorship regardless of bot configuration.

### Action loading errors
Fixed composite actions failing to load due to invalid variable expressions in input descriptions, preventing workflow failures during action initialization.

## ⚙️ Configuration Required

### Bot username configuration
You can now customize the git author for release commits by setting a `bot_username` input. If not provided, commits will use the default `github-actions[bot]` author.

## ⚠️ Breaking Changes

### Authentication input renaming
The `token` and `github_token` inputs have been renamed to `bot_token` for consistency. Update your workflow files to use the new input name:

**Before:**
```yaml
- uses: awinogradov/code-assistants/.github/actions/release-action@v1
  with:
    token: ${{ secrets.GH_TOKEN }}
```

**After:**
```yaml
- uses: awinogradov/code-assistants/.github/actions/release-action@v1
  with:
    bot_token: ${{ secrets.BOT_TOKEN }}
    bot_username: ${{ vars.BOT_USERNAME }}  # optional
```

Also update your repository secrets from `GH_TOKEN` to `BOT_TOKEN` and optionally add a `BOT_USERNAME` variable.

<details><summary>Related issues</summary>

- [#96: Standardize actions on bot_token and bot_username](https://github.com/awinogradov/code-assistants/issues/96)
</details>

### ⚠ BREAKING CHANGES

* **actions:** inputs token and github_token are renamed to bot_token; consumers must
update with: blocks to pass bot_token (and optional bot_username), and workflows now read
secrets.BOT_TOKEN and vars.BOT_USERNAME instead of secrets.GH_TOKEN.

### Features

* **actions:** rename token inputs to bot_token, add bot_username ([160049b](https://github.com/awinogradov/code-assistants/commit/160049b998131e2e5c503559bf5d8e70e7ea8d5a))
* **release-automerge:** gate auto-merge behind release.automerge flag ([dcb51c8](https://github.com/awinogradov/code-assistants/commit/dcb51c87f6026728a9baf6c40837859c3b10d31f))

### Bug Fixes

* **actions:** remove vars expr from descriptions ([f5b2c74](https://github.com/awinogradov/code-assistants/commit/f5b2c74aef1561ca8366ed31da938ef6e7bfb514))
* **release-action:** add per-system ticket tables to monorepo releases ([72e0eb9](https://github.com/awinogradov/code-assistants/commit/72e0eb9599bcebe170a1b54422de811bfe9ff3f9))

### Refactoring
